question.label: Friction scan
question.name: What's been slower or harder than it should have been?
question.description: Invites them to name blockers without framing it as complaint.

answer: The last stretch shipped, but it was heavier than I made it sound.
note: He signaled the work got done but was carrying more hidden weight than he let on, suggesting mild friction and some concealment rather than clean momentum.

question.label: Heavier than said
question.name: When you say 'heavier' — what was making it heavy?
question.description: Drills into the specific source of the load Lin just named so the friction is concrete.

answer: Late nights came from carrying decision bottlenecks and final reviews.
note: Naming late nights from carrying decision bottlenecks and final reviews signals strong over-ownership strain and drag on engagement.

question.label: Decision bottlenecks
question.name: Which decisions are still sticking with you that shouldn't be?
question.description: Drills into the specific decision bottlenecks Lin is still carrying so ownership gaps become concrete.

answer: Some of that work could have moved to the team sooner.
note: He implies ownership should have shifted earlier to the team, signaling a clear delegation and decision-rights gap; [THREAD-DEFERRED] 'moved to the team sooner' not drilled because drill cap reached.

question.label: Opening pace check
question.name: How's the last two weeks actually felt — energy-wise?
question.description: Opener that invites a real answer rather than 'fine'. Reads wellbeing directly.

answer: I am proud of the outcomes, but the pace should not be normal.
note: He explicitly says the recent pace is unsustainable despite pride in the outcomes — strong wellbeing risk with mild positive engagement.

question.label: Thread follow
question.name: proud outcomes, pace — can you say more about what that means for you right now?
question.description: Runtime thread-follow injected because planner did not follow substantive answer.

answer: I still do not get enough substantive feedback on key decisions.
note: Names a specific absence — substantive feedback on key decisions — which signals strain and mild disengagement; also suggests a clarity issue worth probing next.

question.label: Decision feedback
question.name: On key decisions, what feedback are you not getting that would help most?
question.description: Drills into the missing feedback thread to surface the specific gap and what better support would look like.

answer: I want clearer ownership boundaries so I am not default escalation.
note: Names a specific missing condition — clear ownership boundaries so Lin is not the default escalation — which is a strong deficiency-as-request on both load and growth support.

question.label: Priority clarity
question.name: What are you actually focused on this week, and why those things?
question.description: Surfaces whether priorities are crisp or vague; whether they match what you thought.

answer: Next I want to hand off one stream end-to-end and stay out of the middle.
note: He names a concrete next focus and direction of travel — handing off one stream end-to-end to reduce his time in the middle — which is strong clarity, ownership, and stretch signal. [THREAD-DEFERRED-WINDDOWN] 'hand off one stream end-to-end' could be worth unpacking next session.

question.label: Next two weeks
question.name: What would make the next two weeks lighter for you?
question.description: Closes by steering toward practical changes that reduce load without introducing new topics.

answer: If we reset scope now, the next month is sustainable.
note: Resetting scope now as the condition for a sustainable next month signals a practical path to improved load and clearer boundaries.

question.label: Priority ranking
question.name: If you had to cut your work in half tomorrow, what would you drop first — and what's non-negotiable?
question.description: Forces a real priority ranking. Vague answers are a clarity signal.

answer: I want to focus first on unblocking the one dependency that is slowing the team the most this week.
note: Names a specific top priority and its rationale — unblocking the team's main dependency this week — showing strong clarity and some ownership.
